CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Continued To Oct-07-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Voluntarily Dismiss Plaintiffs Paga Claims Without Prejudice And Individual Claims With Prejudice
Off Calendar Jun-06-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Lawrence Freiman, ESQ. of FREIMAN LEGAL Tel:(310) 917-1004 for plaintiff. (via phone)
Michael Christman, ESQ. of Macy Law Department Tel: 314.342.6334 for defendants Macy's, Inc.
Brooke Purcell, ESQ. of SHEPPARD, MULLIN, RICHTER & HAMPTON LLP. Tel: 415.434.9100 for defendant THE FINISH LINE, INC.

Hearing is Held. All counsel appeared at the hearing and contested the entirety of tentative ruling. The matter argued. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Jonathan Bullinger's unopposed Motion to Voluntarily Dismiss Plaintiff's PAGA Claims Without Prejudice and Individual Claims With Prejudice is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Plaintiff seeks a court order under California Rules of Court rule 3.770. The court reads the rule to focus the inquiry on protecting the interests of absent class members (here, absent aggrieved employees) and ensuring fairness based on the record. Plaintiff does not provide sufficient analysis to allow the court to make the necessary findings. For example, Plaintiff concludes that absent aggrieved parties will suffer no prejudice. If the claims are dismissed without prejudice. But what about the passage of time and the statute of limitations? Plaintiff must demonstrate, not simply assert, that absent aggrieved employees will suffer no prejudice. Additionally, Plaintiff asserts he received no consideration for dismissing the representative claim, but Plaintiff must present analysis demonstrating he received no consideration, which generally will require an analysis of his individual claims, their value, the settlement amount, the relation between these figures to show the settlement does not include consideration for dismissing the representative claim; another relevant consideration is the attorney's fees recovery and a showing it does not include consideration for abandoning the representative PAGA claim..

Additionally, this case was filed pursuant to the PAGA statutes, thus the settlement requires court approval under Labor Code section 2699. (See Lab. Code, section 2699(s)(2) ["The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part."].) Here, Plaintiff seeks dismissal of his individual and representative PAGA claims in association with a settlement, but he has not provided a copy of the settlement for the court's review. The court will not approve a settlement it has not reviewed. At a minimum, under section 2699, the court must ensure the interests of the absent aggrieved employees are protected. The court needs to understand the settlement (as discussed in the preceding paragraph) to make this determination. Section 2699(s)(2) establishes another precondition to judicial approval of a PAGA settlement: "The proposed settlement shall be submitted to the agency at the same time that it is submitted to the court." Plaintiff here has not demonstrated that he submitted the proposed settlement to the LWDA. When he filed PAGA claims, Plaintiff accepted the role of public prosecutor and now must make a good faith effort to satisfy the related duties and responsibilities.

Order is Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Joinder In Defendants The Finish Line, Inc.???S And Ericka Hernandez???S Motion To Transfer And Consolidate Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 403 And 1048 (Motions)
Off Calendar Nov-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Lawrence Freiman, Esq. 310-917-1004 Freiman Law for Plaintiff.
John Ellis, Esq. 415-434-9100 Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP for Defendant.
Brian Chun, Esq. 415-357-4600 Lafayette & Kumagai for Defendant Macy's et. al.
Michael Christman, Esq. 324-342-6334 Fro Defendant Macy's et al.

Argued. The Court announces the tentatve ruling. Mr. Freiman contests the tentative ruling. Mr. Ellis, responds and requests the tentative ruling be adopted. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - DEFENDANT MACY'S, INC., FIDELIA MURPHY Joinder In Defendants The Finish Line, Inc.'S And Ericka Hernandez'S Motion To Transfer And Consolidate Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 403 And 1048

Defendants' motion to transfer and consolidate is granted. The Alameda County action (Jonathan Bullinger v. The Finish Line, Inc. (Case 23CV038515)) is transferred to San Francisco and that action is consolidated with this action (CGC-23-605200) for all purposes. All future filings shall be under case number CGC-23-605200.

While plaintiff?'s dismissal of several of his Labor Code claims in this case reduces the overlap, there remains overlap regarding the enforceability of the parties' arbitration agreement. That issue should be resolved in one forum to prevent inconsistent rulings. Defendants demonstrate that the factors listed in CCP 404.1 weigh in their favor. The law abhors the multiplicity of actions and a consolidated proceeding will preserve judicial, party, and witness resources.

Moving party to submit a proposed order approved as to form.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
